prompt: In Software Asset Management, what is the primary reason to normalize software installations into software models?
choices: To make all publishers use the same SKU naming pattern
To map discovered software to a consistent product definition used in compliance and entitlement logic
To automatically create procurement contracts for each installation
To replace discovery data with manually entered install records
explanation: Software models provide the canonical product identity so entitlements, installations, and reconciliation rules can be evaluated consistently.

prompt: A SAM manager wants different editions of a vendor product tracked separately for compliance. Which model design supports this goal best?
choices: Use one software model for all editions and track edition in comments
Create distinct software models that represent edition/version differences relevant to licensing
Disable software model normalization to avoid ambiguity
Store edition information only on contracts
explanation: When licensing rights differ by edition or version, separate software models are required for accurate reconciliation and reporting.

prompt: Which relationship is most important for using software models in effective compliance calculations?
choices: Software model to stockroom location
Software model to incident category
Software model to product catalog image
Software model to entitlement and discovered installation records
explanation: Compliance depends on comparing what is installed against what is licensed, and software models are the shared link across those records.

prompt: Why do SAM administrators review software model governance regularly?
choices: To prevent duplicate or poorly classified models from reducing reconciliation accuracy
To convert all software models into hardware models each quarter
To ensure every model has an attached incident response playbook
To remove publisher information from the model records
explanation: Model governance ensures data quality and prevents duplicate mappings that can distort compliance and optimization insights.

prompt: A discovered software title is unmatched in SAM. What is the recommended next step?
choices: Ignore it until year-end true-up
Delete the discovery record to keep the dashboard clean
Create or map it to the correct software model so future installs normalize correctly
Reclassify it as hardware to avoid compliance exceptions
explanation: Unmatched discoveries should be normalized by updating model mappings so usage and compliance rollups remain accurate.

prompt: In Strategic Portfolio Management, what is the main value of maintaining strategic plans in the platform?
choices: They replace all project and demand records
They provide a structure to define goals, outcomes, and investment alignment across portfolios
They automatically close projects that miss milestones
They eliminate the need for governance boards
explanation: Strategic plans connect objectives to execution and funding decisions so portfolio choices are aligned to enterprise goals.

prompt: A portfolio director needs to show how initiatives support organizational goals. Which configuration supports this best?
choices: Map each investment and initiative to strategic objectives within the strategic plan hierarchy
Track goals only in a slide deck outside ServiceNow
Use cost plans without objective mappings
Create one objective called 'General Improvement' for all work
explanation: Objective-to-investment mappings provide traceability from funded work back to declared strategic intent.

prompt: What planning practice improves confidence in strategic plans during quarterly portfolio reviews?
choices: Freeze all objective metrics for the fiscal year
Use measurable key results and update progress based on portfolio execution data
Only track completed projects, not in-flight initiatives
Prioritize initiatives with the largest teams regardless of value
explanation: Strategic plans are more actionable when objective progress is measured and refreshed from current execution data.

prompt: Which scenario best demonstrates strategic plan governance in SPM?
choices: Projects are approved solely by submission date
Objective owners review plan progress and adjust priorities before new funding decisions
All demand records are auto-approved without scoring
Resource plans are hidden from portfolio managers
explanation: Governance involves periodic review of objective progress and using that evidence to steer funding and prioritization.

prompt: A PMO wants to reduce misalignment between strategy and delivery. What should they enforce first?
choices: Require each major initiative to reference an approved strategic objective and expected outcome
Allow teams to create initiatives without objective links for speed
Disable strategic planning modules in non-production instances
Track strategic themes only in financial planning tools
explanation: Mandatory alignment of initiatives to approved objectives improves prioritization discipline and outcome reporting.
